sn4.25:1.2: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went to Māra the Evil One and, on arrival, addressed him in verse:
sn4.25:2.2: For which man do you grieve?
sn4.25:2.5: with a snare of passion—will bring him to you.
sn4.25:3.1: Māra: “An arahant, well-gone in the world,
sn4.25:3.2: isn’t easily brought in by passion.
sn4.25:3.4: That’s why I’m so violently sad.”
sn4.25:4.1: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said to him,
sn4.25:4.2: “We serve you at your feet, contemplative.”
sn4.25:4.3: But the Blessed One paid them no attention, as he was released in the unexcelled ending of acquisitions.
sn4.25:5.1: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went off to one side and considered,
sn4.25:5.3: Why don’t we each assume the form of a hundred girls?”
sn4.25:5.4: So Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—having each assumed the form of a hundred girls, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said to him,
sn4.25:5.5: “We serve you at your feet, contemplative.”
sn4.25:5.6: Again, the Blessed One paid them no attention, as he was released in the unexcelled ending of acquisitions.
sn4.25:6.1: So Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went off to one side and considered,
sn4.25:6.2: “Many & varied are the appetites of men.
sn4.25:6.3: Why don’t we each assume the form of a hundred women who have never given birth?”
sn4.25:6.4: So Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—having each assumed the form of a hundred women who had never given birth, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said to him,
sn4.25:6.5: “We serve you at your feet, contemplative.”
sn4.25:6.6: Again, the Blessed One paid them no attention, as he was released in the unexcelled ending of acquisitions.
sn4.25:7.1: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went off to one side and considered, “Many & varied are the appetites of men.
sn4.25:7.2: Why don’t we each assume the form of a hundred women who have borne one child …
sn4.25:8.2: a hundred women who have borne two children …
sn4.25:9.4: a hundred older women?” So Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—having each assumed the form of a hundred older women, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said to him, “We serve you at your feet, contemplative.”
sn4.25:9.5: Again, the Blessed One paid them no attention, as he was released in the unexcelled ending of acquisitions.
sn4.25:9.6: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went off to one side and said,
sn4.25:9.7: “Wow, it’s true what our father said:
sn4.25:10.2: isn’t easily brought in by passion.
sn4.25:10.4: That’s why I’m so violently sad.’
sn4.25:11.1: “Any contemplative or brahman who hadn’t gone beyond passion, if we were to approach him with this approach: His heart would split or he would cough up hot blood or he would go mad, out of his mind.
sn4.25:11.2: Just as a green reed cut down would dry out, wither, & fade,
sn4.25:11.3: in the same way, he would dry out, wither, & fade.”
sn4.25:12.1: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, stood to one side.
sn4.25:12.2: As she was standing there, Māra’s daughter Craving addressed the Blessed One with verses:
sn4.25:13.1: “Practicing jhāna in the forest—are you overcome with grief?
sn4.25:13.2: Have you lost, or do you desire, wealth?
sn4.25:13.3: Have you done something blameworthy in the village?
sn4.25:13.4: Is it because you become intimate people,
sn4.25:13.5: but intimacy doesn’t prosper for you with anyone at all?”
sn4.25:14.2: peace of the heart—having defeated the army of forms endearing & alluring,
sn4.25:14.3: practicing jhāna alone, I awakened to bliss.
sn4.25:14.4: That’s why I don’t become intimate with people,
sn4.25:14.5: why intimacy doesn’t prosper with me when made by anyone at all.”
sn4.25:15.1: Then Māra’s daughter Discontent addressed the Blessed One in verse:
sn4.25:16.1: “How does a monk here often dwell,
sn4.25:16.2: who has crossed the five floods and also the sixth?
sn4.25:16.3: How, while cultivating jhāna, does he keep perceptions of sensuality far away?”
sn4.25:17.1: The Buddha: “Calmed in body, well released in mind,
sn4.25:18.1: he’s not provoked, doesn’t flow, isn’t slothful:
sn4.25:18.2: Like this a monk here often dwells,
sn4.25:18.3: who has crossed the five floods and also the sixth.
sn4.25:18.4: Cultivating jhāna like this, he keeps perceptions of sensuality far away.”
sn4.25:19.1: Then Māra’s daughter Passion addressed the Blessed One in verse:
sn4.25:20.1: “Cutting off craving, practicing with a group:
sn4.25:20.3: O, how this homeless one, having snatched them
sn4.25:20.4: from the King of Death, will lead a great crowd away.”
sn4.25:21.2: do lead with the true Dhamma.
sn4.25:21.4: leading by Dhamma, what’s the jealousy?”
sn4.25:22.1: Then Māra’s daughters—Craving, Discontent, & Passion—went to Māra the Evil One.
sn4.25:22.2: He saw them coming from afar and,
sn4.25:22.3: on seeing them, addressed them with verses:
sn4.25:23.1: “Fools! Cleave a mountain with a lily-stalk.
sn4.25:23.3: Dig up a crag with your fingernails.
sn4.25:23.4: Chew on iron with your teeth.
sn4.25:24.1: Raising a rock above your head,
sn4.25:24.2: go over a cliff, into a pit.
sn4.25:24.3: Like striking your chest on a stump—
sn4.25:25.3: but the Teacher blew them off,
sn4.25:25.4: like the wind, a fallen tuft of cotton.
